ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
gid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gidar.
git: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gitxsan.
him: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Himachali languages.
hin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hindi.
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
mind: noun. The capability for rational thought.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
night: noun. The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
nth: adjective. Occurring at position n in a sequence.
thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
thing: noun. That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
